questions.stem: Eight persons A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. B sits third to the left of D. F sits second to the right of B. C sits between A and B. H sits second to the left of A. E is not an immediate neighbour of D. Who sits between E and D?

Six people P, Q, R, S, T and U are sitting in a row facing north. Q sits at one of the extreme ends. T has only one neighbour. S is not a neighbour of Q. R sits third to the left of U. Who sits between R and T?

Five friends A, B, C, D and E are sitting on a bench. A is sitting next to B. C is sitting next to D. D is not sitting with E. E is on the left end. C is on second position from right. Who is sitting at the centre?

Seven persons are sitting in a straight line facing north. C sits fourth from the left end. B sits second to the right of C. Only one person sits between D and B. A sits third to the left of D. E is an immediate neighbour of A. F sits second to the left of E. Who sits at the extreme right end?

Eight people are sitting around a square table with two on each side. Persons sitting on opposite sides face each other. A sits second to the left of B. C faces A. D is to the immediate right of C. E faces D. F is to the immediate left of E. G and H sit on the same side. Who faces B?

Six persons M, N, O, P, Q and R are sitting in two rows with three persons in each row. Persons in row 1 face south and persons in row 2 face north. M is at one of the extreme ends and faces O. Q is to the immediate right of O. P faces N. R is not at any extreme end. Who is sitting between M and P?

In a certain arrangement, five boxes are stacked one above another. The red box is above the green but below the blue. The yellow box is just below the green. The white box is at the bottom. Which box is at the top?

Eight persons A through H sit around a circular table. Some face centre, some face outside. B sits third to the right of F. C sits opposite to B. A and D are immediate neighbours of C. G sits second to left of A. E is not a neighbour of G. H faces outside. How many persons face outside?

Four couples are sitting in a row. No husband sits adjacent to his wife. A is the husband of B. C is the wife of D. E sits at one extreme end. F sits third from E. G is not adjacent to E or F. B sits adjacent to D. Who sits between C and A?

Ten students are sitting in two rows with five in each. Row 1 faces south and row 2 faces north. P sits at one extreme end of row 1 and faces Q. R sits second to the left of Q. T sits opposite to the one who is third from the left in row 1. S sits at an extreme end of row 2. How many students sit between R and S?
Statements: All dogs are cats. Some cats are rats. No rat is a cow. Conclusions: I. Some dogs are rats. II. No cow is a dog. Which conclusion follows?
Statements: Some books are pens. All pens are chairs. Some chairs are tables. Conclusions: I. Some books are chairs. II. Some tables are pens. Which conclusion follows?
Statements: No flower is a tree. All trees are plants. Some plants are herbs. Conclusions: I. No flower is a plant. II. Some herbs are trees. Which conclusion follows?
Statements: All squares are circles. All circles are triangles. No triangle is a rectangle. Conclusions: I. No square is a rectangle. II. All squares are triangles. Which conclusion follows?
Statements: Some rivers are lakes. All lakes are oceans. No ocean is a pond. Conclusions: I. Some rivers are oceans. II. No lake is a pond. Which conclusion follows?
Statements: All mangoes are apples. Some apples are oranges. All oranges are bananas. Conclusions: I. Some mangoes are oranges. II. Some bananas are apples. Which conclusion follows?
Statements: No car is a bus. Some buses are trains. All trains are planes. Conclusions: I. Some planes are buses. II. No car is a train. Which conclusion follows?
Statements: All metals are liquids. All liquids are gases. No gas is a solid. Conclusions: I. No metal is a solid. II. All metals are gases. III. Some gases are metals. Which conclusions follow?
Statements: Some tables are chairs. No chair is a window. All windows are doors. Conclusions: I. Some tables are not windows. II. All doors being chairs is a possibility. Which conclusion follows?
Statements: All kings are queens. No queen is a prince. Some princes are knights. Conclusions: I. No king is a prince. II. Some knights are not queens. Which conclusion follows?
In a certain code, COMPUTER is written as RFUVQNPC. How will MEDICINE be written in that code?
If TRAIN is coded as 72, and BUS is coded as 38, how is CAR coded?
In a code language, 'sky is blue' is written as 'da pa ta', 'blue is nice' is written as 'ta na da'. What is the code for 'blue'?
In a certain code, MOBILE is written as OMDGNK. How is GALAXY written?
In a code language, 'go for picnic' is written as '5#9 3#6 7#2', 'picnic is fun' is written as '7#2 4#5 2#3'. What is the code for 'picnic'?
If A=1, B=2... Z=26, and MEAN is coded as 43, what is the code for MODE?
In a certain code, FORGET is written as DPPHCR. How will MEMORY be written?
In a code language, 'she likes chocolates' means 'tic pic nic', 'he likes games' means 'nic sic vic', 'she plays games' means 'vic tic wic'. What is the code for 'likes'?
HOUSE is coded as JQWUG in a certain pattern. How is CHAIR coded in the same pattern?
In a code language, PENCIL is written as RGPEKN. How is ERASER written?
Pointing to a man in a photograph, a woman says 'His mother's son's wife is my mother-in-law.' How is the woman related to the man in the photograph?
A is the father of B. C is the daughter of B. D is the brother of B. E is the son of D. How is C related to E?
Introducing a girl, Ravi said 'She is the only daughter of the father of my mother.' How is the girl related to Ravi?
P is the brother of Q. R is the daughter of P. S is the sister of R. T is the mother of P. How is Q related to S?
M is married to N. N is the sister of O. O is the father of P. Q is the mother of M. How is P related to M?
Pointing to a photograph, Rajesh says 'The person in this photo is the brother of the sister of my father's son.' Who is in the photograph?
A woman introduces a man as 'the son of the brother of my mother.' How is the man related to the woman?
X says to Y, 'Your mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is X related to Y?
A + B means A is the father of B. A - B means A is the wife of B. A x B means A is the brother of B. If P + Q - R x S, how is S related to P?
In a family of six persons, F is the sister of D. B is the brother of E. D is the daughter of A. A is the father of B. E is the son of C. How is C related to F?
A man walks 5 km south, then turns left and walks 3 km east, then turns left and walks 5 km north. How far is he from the starting point?
Ravi starts from point A, walks 10 m north, turns right walks 15 m, turns right walks 10 m, turns left walks 5 m. How far and in which direction is he from point A?
From his house, Rahul goes 15 km towards North. Then he turns left and goes 10 km. He again turns left and goes 15 km. In which direction is he facing now?
A person walks 4 km towards east, then turns left and walks 6 km, then turns right and walks 4 km. He is in which direction from the starting point?
Meena walks 20 m north, then 15 m east, then 20 m south, then 25 m west. How far is she from the starting point and in which direction?
Point B is 10 m south of point A. Point C is 5 m east of B. Point D is 3 m north of C. Point E is 7 m west of D. How far is E from A?
Suresh walked 6 m facing the rising sun. He turned to his left and walked 4 m. Again he turned left and walked 3 m. How far is he from the starting point?
A man starts from point O, walks 8 km south, turns left walks 6 km, turns left walks 8 km, and turns right walks 2 km. What is the shortest distance from O?
Rekha starts from her home facing north. She walks 3 km, turns right and walks 4 km, turns right and walks 7 km, turns left and walks 2 km. In which direction is she from home?
Starting from point P, Ram walks 12 m towards south. He turns left and walks 8 m. He then turns left and walks 5 m. What is the shortest distance between his final position and point P?
Statements: A > B >= C, D < C, E >= A. Conclusions: I. E > D  II. B >= D. Which is true?
Statements: P <= Q = R > S, T >= R. Conclusions: I. T > S  II. P <= T. Which is true?
Statements: M > N = O >= P, Q < N. Conclusions: I. M > Q  II. O >= Q. Which conclusion follows?
Statements: H = I > J, K >= I, L < J. Conclusions: I. K > L  II. H >= K. Which is true?
Statements: A >= B > C = D, E < C. Conclusions: I. A > E  II. B > E  III. D >= E. Which is/are true?
Statements: X < Y = Z >= W > V. Conclusions: I. Y > V  II. X < W. Which is true?
Statements: L > M >= N, O = M, P < N. Conclusions: I. L > P  II. O >= P  III. L > O. Which is/are true?
Statements: D >= E > F = G, H < F. Conclusions: I. D > H  II. E > H. Which is true?
Statements: J = K > L >= M, N > K. Conclusions: I. N > M  II. J < N  III. N > L. Which is/are true?
Statements: R <= S = T > U >= V, W > T. Conclusions: I. W > V  II. R <= W  III. S < W. Which is/are true?

A machine rearranges words and numbers in steps. Input: 'go 52 now 17 far 93 his 41'. Step I: 'far go 52 now 17 93 his 41'. Step II: 'far 93 go 52 now 17 his 41'. Following this pattern, what would Step IV be?
Input: 'over 39 cat 27 big 63 run 18'. Step I: 'big over 39 cat 27 63 run 18'. Step II: 'big 63 over 39 cat 27 run 18'. What will Step III be?
A machine arranges numbers in ascending order from left, one at a time. Input: '45 12 78 34 56 23'. How many steps are needed to get the final arrangement?
Input: 'win 58 23 top 71 cup 36 pen'. In each step the machine arranges one word alphabetically from left and one number in descending order alternately. What is Step III output?
Input: '9 2 5 7 3 6 1 8'. The machine swaps adjacent elements if left > right in each pass (bubble sort). How many complete passes are needed to fully sort this?
Input: 'joy red top aim 67 43 29 85'. Step I: 'aim joy red top 67 43 29 85'. Step II: 'aim 85 joy red top 67 43 29'. What is Step IV?

In a certain machine, each step reverses a progressively larger segment from the left. Input: '1 2 3 4 5'. Step I reverses first 2: '2 1 3 4 5'. Step II reverses first 3: '3 1 2 4 5'. What is Step IV?

Input: 'max 19 27 cut 41 dog 8 fan'. The machine alternately picks the smallest number and earliest alphabetical word. Step I: '8 max 19 27 cut 41 dog fan'. Step II: '8 cut max 19 27 41 dog fan'. What is Step IV?
Input: '14 5 31 8 22 11'. Each step moves the largest unsorted number to the right end. After Step 3, the arrangement is:
Input: 'sun 45 old 12 new 78 big 33'. Machine logic: even steps pick the highest number to right of last arranged element; odd steps pick next alphabetical word to left. What is Step II?
Is X the brother of Y? Statement I: X is the son of Z. Statement II: Y is the daughter of Z.
Who among A, B, C, D, E is the tallest? Statement I: B is taller than A and C. Statement II: D is taller than B but shorter than E.
What is the code for 'sky' in a code language? Statement I: 'sky is blue' is coded as 'pa ta na'. Statement II: 'blue sky high' is coded as 'na da pa'.
In which direction is town B from town A? Statement I: Town C is to the north of A and to the west of B. Statement II: Town D is to the east of A and south of B.
How is P related to Q? Statement I: R is the mother of P. Statement II: Q is the son of R.
On which day of the week did Mohan visit Delhi? Statement I: Mohan visited Delhi two days before Friday. Statement II: Mohan did not visit Delhi on Monday or Tuesday.
Is A greater than B? Statement I: A is greater than C. Statement II: B is less than C.
What is Ravi's rank from the top in a class of 50? Statement I: Ravi is 5 ranks below Sunil who is 20th from the top. Statement II: Ravi is 26 ranks above the last.
How many daughters does Mr. Sharma have? Statement I: Mrs. Sharma is the mother of two children. Statement II: Ram is the only son of Mr. Sharma.
Among five persons sitting in a row, who is in the middle? Statement I: A and B are at extreme ends. Statement II: C is between A and D.
In a row of 40 students, Ravi is 7th from the left end. What is his position from the right end?
In a class, Mohan's rank from top is 15th and from bottom is 25th. How many students are in the class?
Suresh is 7 ranks ahead of Mohan who ranks 28th from the last. If there are 50 students, what is Suresh's rank from the start?
In a row of children, Nita is 14th from the left and Mita is 7th from the right. If they interchange their positions, Nita becomes 23rd from the left. How many children are there?
In a class of 60 students, A's rank is 17th from the top. B is 10 ranks below A. What is B's rank from the bottom?
Arun is 5th from the top and Sunil is 24th from the bottom in a class. If Arun is 6 ranks above Sunil, how many students are in the class?
In a row of boys, Ramu is 18th from the left end and Somu is 18th from the right end. If they interchange their positions, Ramu becomes 25th from the left. How many boys are in the row?
A is 7th from the left and B is 12th from the right. If there are 5 persons between them, what is the minimum number of persons in the row?
In a queue, Ram is 10th from the front and Shyam is 25th from the end. If Ram is exactly in the middle of the queue, what is Shyam's position from the front?
In a class of 45 students, Mohan is ranked 20th from the top. Sohan is 5 ranks below Mohan. What is Sohan's rank from the bottom?
What comes next in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
Find the missing term: AZ, BY, CX, DW, ?
What comes next: A2C, E4G, I6K, M8O, ?
Find the wrong term: 3, 7, 15, 31, 64, 127
What comes next in the series: ZA5, YB10, XC15, WD20, ?
Find the next term: B2E, D4H, F6K, H8N, ?
What is wrong in the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, 24, 36?
Complete the series: AC3, CE5, EG7, GI9, ?
Find the next term: 2A, 4D, 8G, 16J, ?
What replaces the question mark: Z1A, X2C, V3E, T4G, ?

Statement: The government has decided to levy a heavy penalty on industries polluting rivers. Assumption I: Industries may stop polluting rivers. Assumption II: The government has the authority to penalise industries.

Statement: 'Apply immediately for the post of manager. Only graduates need apply.' Assumption I: Only graduates are capable of handling managerial work. Assumption II: There will be enough graduate applicants.
Statement: The school has decided to increase the academic hours by 30 minutes. Which of the following strengthens this decision?
options: Longer hours improve test scores in similar schools

questions.stem: Statement: Despite being costly, people prefer branded clothes over local ones. Assumption: Branded clothes are of better quality than local clothes.

Statement: The company decided to hire contract workers for the project. Course of action I: The company should abolish permanent positions. Course of action II: The company should review work quality periodically.
Statement: 'No ticket without ID proof' – notice at railway counter. Assumption I: People usually have ID proof. Assumption II: Only those with tickets should enter the train.
Statement: Country X has banned the import of electronic toys from Country Y. Which weakens this decision?
options: Country X has no domestic toy manufacturers
Country X has sufficient domestic production

questions.stem: Statement: Many people in the city have shifted to cycling to commute. Conclusion I: Cycling is healthier than driving. Conclusion II: The city infrastructure supports cycling.
Statement: The airline has announced a 50% discount on fares for the next month. Assumption: People will book more flights due to the discount.

Statement: The municipality has decided to demolish all illegal constructions. Which assumption is implicit? I. The municipality has identified illegal constructions. II. Citizens will not resist the demolition drive.
